title: Australian Capital Territory (ACT)
description:
11 mars
Job available on market garden/farm
What We Are Looking For:
We are seeking a hardworking, physically fit, and reliable person to join us on our small, family owned market garden near Bungendore NSW. Our garden is certified organic/biodynamic and produces beautiful food while caring for the land. If you’re motivated by sustainable agriculture and enjoy working outdoors, this could be the role for you!
Duties:
Your responsibilities will vary by season and include, but are not limited to:
Weeding beds in the market garden and chipping out serrated tussock in paddocks.
Planting and harvesting crops (broad range of seasonal veggies and strawberries).
Garden maintenance, including assisting with irrigation bed preparation and mowing.
Assisting with farm infrastructure projects such as fencing, building repairs, and equipment maintenance.
General farm labour on both the garden and broader farm operations as required.
Must Haves:
A strong work ethic and physical fitness to handle manual labor. Tasks may involve repetitive actions such as bending and kneeling.
Reliability and commitment to working in all weather conditions.
Your own vehicle to get to and from the job site.
Non-smoker status (required for biosecurity reasons).
Ongoing availability for 3-5 days per week, must include Monday and Friday.
A proactive attitude and ability to work both independently for extended periods or with others.
Preferable:
Previous experience in farming or gardening.
Knowledge of organic farming practices.
What We Offer:
A rewarding role in a beautiful outdoor setting.
Opportunities to expand your skills in organic farming and landcare.
Potential for this role to expand for the right candidate.
If you would like to work for our family farm please send a brief paragraph about yourself and experience to gumtree. If the job ad is live on gumtree, the position is still available. We will only respond to shortlisted candidates. Looking forward to hearing from you.
https://www.gumtree.com.au/web/listing/labourer/1340930185

title: Australian Capital Territory (ACT)
description:
9 déc. 2025
Farm Hand (Insect farming)
ABOUT US
At Goterra, we believe climate change is the crisis of our time. We exist to help solve it. We're reimagining a future where nature and automation work in tandem to solve complex problems like reducing food waste and securing our food supply chain.
We are the change we want to see in the world. We strive and push as hard as we can for our economy and environment to reap the benefits of our dedicated research, relentless testing and state-of-the-art automated robotic infrastructure.
We process food waste using insects and turn this into to by products, animal protein and high grade fertilizer. And we do this, onsite, in modular, mobile insect farms.
We’re fast moving, agile and collaborative and we work relentlessly to make the world better, Billions of Tonnes Better.
ABOUT THE ROLE
We're a small, ambitious team working on ground breaking technology and we’re seeking motivated people who aren't afraid of rolling their sleeves up. Supporting our great cause is the Production team, responsible for breeding and hatching Black Soldier Fly larvae for the purpose of breaking down organic food waste.
As a Farm Hand your job is to ensure this process is as efficient as possible, nurturing and growing our larvae to an ideal state. You will be working in a small team that work on a rolling 4 days on / 4 days off roster, set up to ensure we have daily oversight of the farm. If you’re seeking to engage in a role that contributes to the advancement of sustainable farming systems, and are interested in a genuine career opportunity with a fast moving, innovative company then this position is for you.
MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES
Attend to farming duties on a rolling roster supporting efficient growth of larvae
Monitor and control the system to ensure ideal conditions of farm are upheld
Assist with the insect breeding cycle through the research of genetics, nutrition and domestication
Follow instructions and guidelines from shift leader to ensure smooth operations
Managing production, growth targets and health and wellbeing of our growing commercial colony in consultation with the team
Assisting all members of the production teams in daily operations.
Collaborate with other departments to ensure company goals can be met
Provide day-to-day data entry for accuracy and efficiency of operation
Collection and processing of insects through the life cycle
Conduct sampling for quality assurance and testing,
Diligently attend to daily cleaning and hygiene duties
Cleaning and maintaining work areas and equipment
Support maintenance and upkeep of facilities and equipment
KEY SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE
A strong understanding of process work and importance of following procedures in a controlled environment
Previous experience in a manual operator role
Comfortable working with larvae/insects/flies
Previous experience with insect farming is NOT a requirement (we'll teach you what you need to know)
Be passionate about their profession, take pride in their work and extract great satisfaction from completing innovative solutions that advance objectives.
Strong attention to detail
Physically capable of manual labour and ability to lift multiple trays weighing more than 10kgs
The capability to multitask effectively under pressure.
A team player attitude, sharing information and assisting others to achieve common objectives.
Additional role requirements:
Weekend work and reasonable overtime is required
Be prepared to support the business as part of a small team.
We pride ourselves on being an inclusive organisation and encourage applications from anyone with passion for our mission, particularly from women, veterans, people with disability, the LGBTIQ+ community, and people from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds
Please apply via our website:
https://goterra.au/careers/

SECTION WAITER / BARTENDER – LUNETTA & LUNETTA TRATTORIA
SECTION WAITER / BARTENDER – LUNETTA & LUNETTA TRATTORIA
Location: Red Hill, Canberra
Employment Type: Casual
Join Our Team
Lunetta and Lunetta Trattoria are two of Canberra’s most exciting dining destinations, celebrated for exceptional service, expertly crafted cocktails, and a passion for food and wine. We are seeking versatile and enthusiastic hospitality professionals who can confidently take on both section waiter and bartender duties in a fast-paced, team-focused environment.
What We’re Looking For
We want team members who bring energy, confidence, and a passion for service—whether you’re behind the bar mixing cocktails or managing a section on the floor. Experience is ideal, but a positive attitude and willingness to learn are just as important.
What You’ll Be Doing
- Managing your own section and providing attentive, professional table service
- Taking orders, serving food and drinks, and anticipating guest needs
- Crafting classic and signature cocktails with precision and consistency
- Recommending food and beverage pairings to enhance the dining experience
- Maintaining a clean, organised bar and section throughout service
- Collaborating with team members to ensure seamless service
- Upholding RSA and responsible service standards
What You’ll Need
- Experience in table service and/or bar work preferred
- Knowledge of food, wine, and classic cocktails
- Excellent communication and customer service skills
- Ability to thrive in busy service periods
- Availability to work nights, weekends and public holidays
- RSA certification

- A chance to be part of a respected and growing hospitality group
Apply Now

title: Australian Capital Territory (ACT)
description:
8 mai 2025
Maintenance & Facilities Assistant
Job Title: Maintenance & Facilities Assistant
Location: Canberra Accommodation Centre, ACT
Employment Type: Casual
About Us
Canberra Accommodation Centre provides long stay, hotel and group accommodation, in the heart of the ACT. We pride ourselves on offering a clean, safe, and well-maintained environment for our guests. We are looking for a hands-on Maintenance & Facilities Assistant to support the upkeep of our facilities, ensuring a high standard of cleanliness, safety, and functionality.
About the Role
As a Maintenance & Facilities Assistant, you will be responsible for general maintenance, repairs, and cleaning tasks to ensure our accommodation and event spaces are in top condition. This role is ideal for someone who is proactive, reliable, and has a good mix of maintenance skills and general cleaning experience.
Key Responsibilities
Perform routine maintenance and minor repairs across the facility, including plumbing, painting, carpentry, and general handyman tasks.
Conduct regular inspections to identify maintenance issues and report them as needed.
Assist with basic electrical, HVAC, and plumbing troubleshooting (with external contractors engaged for complex issues).
Ensure all common areas, guest rooms, and amenities are clean, tidy, and in excellent condition.
Perform general cleaning tasks such as vacuuming, mopping, dusting, and rubbish removal.
Set up and pack down event spaces as required.
Monitor and maintain stock levels of cleaning and maintenance supplies.
Follow workplace health and safety (WHS) regulations to ensure a safe environment for guests and staff.
Respond to maintenance and cleaning requests in a timely manner.
Assist in emergency situations, such as power outages or plumbing issues, by providing initial troubleshooting.
About You
We’re looking for someone with:
✅ Previous experience in general maintenance, handyman work, or cleaning roles.
✅ Basic knowledge of plumbing, electrical work, painting, and carpentry.
✅ The ability to work independently and proactively identify issues.
✅ A strong focus on cleanliness, hygiene, and attention to detail.
✅ Good communication skills and a friendly, helpful attitude.
✅ Physical fitness, as the role requires manual handling and moving between different areas of the site.
✅ An understanding of workplace health and safety regulations.
✅ A flexible approach to work, including availability for occasional weekend shifts if needed.
Preferred but Not Essential
Trade qualifications or certifications in maintenance-related fields.
Experience working in hospitality, accommodation, or facilities management.
Applicants that are bilingual or able to speak Dzongkha would be an advantage but not essential.
What We Offer
⭐ A friendly and supportive work environment.
⭐ Competitive hourly rate or salary (depending on experience).
⭐ Opportunities to develop your skills across maintenance and cleaning.
How to Apply
If you have the skills and experience to keep our facilities running smoothly, we’d love to hear from you!
Send your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your experience to sjg@urbanstays.au

description:
8 nov. 2023
Waste Pick Up - Queanbeyan Depot - Canberra
If you enjoy working outdoors and don't mind doing waste collection then this is the role for you!
Your new company
Our client is a local council who are seeking an ongoing worker to work starting from their Queanbeyan depot.
Your new role
In this position you will work 6am to 3pm Monday to Friday. You will go out with another employee in the waste truck and collect and empty rubbish bins from parks and public areas. You will also be required to pick up rubbish on roadsides to keep areas looking clean and tidy and maybe also assist with operating the forklift in the yard to unload deliveries and maintain the depot by occasionally using the whipper snipper.
What you'll need to succeed
This role needs someone with a can do attitude who isn't afraid of getting their hands dirty! You will be supplied with PPE but will need to be ok with consistently working with rubbish. Aside from having a good attitude and being reliable you will need;
A drivers license and reliable transport to get to work
A MR licence or higher is advantageous
A forklift licence is beneficial
The ability to spend most of the day walking and picking up /emptying bins
What you'll get in return
If successful you will be paid an hourly rate of $32.26 per hour plus super. You will have stable work hours in a long term position.
What you need to do now
If you are interested in this position and feel that you have the skills described to be successful, then submit your interest in this role by clicking 'Apply Now' and following the prompts or emailing your CV and the job title to Ella Vuckovic at Ella.Vuckovic@hays.com.au or contact me on 0498 003 486

description:
4 j
Casual Cafe Chef
POSITION: Casual Cafe Chef
LOCATION: Narre Warren VIC
SALARY: $33.50 per hour weekdays | $41.88 Saturday | $48.59 Sunday
ABOUT THE BUSINESS
CrustUp Pty Ltd is a family-owned company based in Narre Warren in the process of acquiring a well-established and busy breakfast and lunch cafe in Berwick.
ABOUT THE ROLE
We are looking for an experienced cafe chef to join our team for a minimum of 5 days per week (must be available Monday to Wednesday) — approximately 34 hours per week. Hours are 7am to 2pm daily. No late nights.
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
— Preparing and cooking full breakfast and lunch menu to a consistent high standard
— Running full kitchen service independently during your shift
— Managing daily kitchen prep, opening, and closing procedures
— Monitoring food quality presentation and portion consistency
— Maintaining food safety and hygiene standards
— Contributing to menu development and kitchen improvement
— Managing stock and communicating ordering needs to management
REQUIREMENTS
— Minimum 2 years experience in a cafe or breakfast and lunch kitchen
— Confident running a full service independently
— Strong with brunch dishes—eggs Benedict smashed avo smoothie bowls, pasta, and cabinet items
— Food Safety Supervisor certificate or willingness to obtain
— Available weekends—essential
— Reliable motivated and takes pride in food quality
— Local to City of Casey or southeast Melbourne preferred
WHAT WE OFFER
— $33.50 per hour weekdays — $41.88 Saturday — $48.59 Sunday
— Performance-based incentives for the right person
— Free meals every shift
— Full creative input into the kitchen and menu
— Supportive family environment
— Daytime hours only — home every afternoon
— Long-term opportunity with genuine growth potential
WORK RIGHTS
Must have full unrestricted work rights. We welcome citizens, permanent residents, partner visa holders, graduate visa 485 with a minimum of 12 months remaining, and working holiday visa holders with a minimum of 12 months remaining. No student visas. No sponsorship available.
TO APPLY
Email crustupoven@gmail.com with a brief introduction, your experience, and your current visa status. We respond to every application within 24 hours.

title: Victoria (VIC)
description:
30 mars
Job Ag machine operator
Machine Operator – Stephens Ag Services
Location: Camperdown, Victoria (fieldwork across region and surrounds)
Stephens Ag Services is a local agricultural contracting business based in Camperdown, specialising in hay and silage making, Tow and Fert liquid ferapplication, conventional spraying, manure/fert spreading, ground cultivation, and supporting dairy and grazing farms in the area.
We’re on the lookout for a reliable, hands-on Machine Operator to join the team and help deliver top-quality service to our clients.
About the Role:
• Safely and efficiently operate agricultural machinery (tractors, sprayers, Tow and Fert systems, hay/silage equipment, spreaders, cultivators, and other implements).
• Carry out paddock tasks including spraying (liquid and granular), fertilising, hay/silage operations, ground prep, manure spreading, and general contracting work.
• Perform routine machine checks, basic maintenance, cleaning, and setup to keep equipment running smoothly.
• Work independently or with the small team to meet client needs across the region.
Hours & Commitment:
• Part-time to start (flexible hours/days depending on seasonal demands and your availability).
• Strong potential to become full-time for the right person as workloads grow (especially during peaks like spraying, hay, and silage seasons).
• Expect variable hours during busy periods – outdoor work in varying weather.
What We’re Looking For:
• Current Australian driver’s licence (essential – manual experience preferred for tractors/implements).
• Experience operating farm machinery (tractors, sprayers, Tow and Fert, hay equipment, etc.) – valued highly, but not essential if you’re eager to learn with a solid work ethic.
• Safety-minded, reliable, physically capable for fieldwork, and comfortable working outdoors.
• Positive attitude, good communication, and ability to work solo or in a team.
• Based locally or willing to travel within the Colac–Camperdown–Warrnambool area.
What We Offer:
• Competitive hourly rate (based on experience and skills).
• Path to full-time employment and ongoing seasonal/regular work for the right fit.
• Hands-on role in a supportive, local ag contracting setup.
• Chance to build skills with modern equipment like Tow and Fert systems in a dairy-focused region.
If you’re keen on ag contracting, enjoy machinery work, and want to be part of a growing local business in the Camperdown area, get in touch!
How to Apply:
Send your resume and a quick note about your experience (especially any machinery operation) to Josh via:
• stephensagservices@gmail.com
Applications ongoing – position filled when we find the right person.

title: Victoria (VIC)
description:
27 mars
Farm Hands - 2x Positions Available Merbein & Sunraysia Region
Farm Hands - 2x Positions Available Merbein & Nangiloc VIC
Sunraysia Region VIC
Casual Positions
OVERVIEW:
Southern Cross Farms (SC Farms) provides horticultural management services to private, institutional and corporate investment groups. With several production sites across Sunraysia, and operations in the Riverland and Riverina regions, we are seeking a suitably experienced farm hand with for each of our farm sites located at Merbein & Nangiloc, VIC.
Reporting to the applicable Farm Manager, the role/s provide an opportunity for the successful applicant/s to further develop their operational experience in horticultural production.
JOB DESCRIPTION:
Duties include but are not limited to:
Tractor work, such as slashing & mulching.
Operate and maintain herbicide spray equipment.
Assist with chemical and fertigation applications.
Flexibility to work extra hours during harvest season when required.
Maintain, repair and complete basic level maintenance on farm machinery.
Monitoring irrigation equipment/systems.
Repairs and maintenance of irrigation lines and pumps.
Ensure irrigation application is adequate to support vine growth
Develop a strong working environment and relationships with Farm Management, farm staff and contractors
Liaise with Farm Manager on all farming practices
Maintain compliance with workplace health and safety, HR policies & food safety requirements.
Manage and report risks and incidents in accordance with SC Farms OHS policies and procedures.
QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS:
Cert II in Horticulture (is desirable but not essential)
Current Australian Driver's license (essential)
Be able to work in a team environment with other staff
Be self-motivated and able to communicate effectively with other team members.
Formal training and/or experience in the following areas will be highly regarded:
Demonstrated hands-on experience in table grape production
Pest and disease identification and control
Irrigation system management
Machinery operation
Food hygiene
Farm Safety
First Aid
Forklift License
Chemical Handling Certificate
EXPERIENCE:
Practical experience with horticultural production would be an asset, preferably in table grape operations.
REMUNERATION/CONDITIONS:
An attractive remuneration package will be offered subject to the applicant's experience, skills and qualifications.
The position will include a 6 month probation period unless the successful applicant is an existing employee within the SC Farms Group..
New employees will be required to pass a Pre-Employment Medical and Drug & Alcohol screening.
HOW TO APPLY:
To apply for this position please email a copy of your current resume to: admin@scfarms.com.au

Grain Handlers - Nullawil, VIC
The Role
We are seeking Grain Handlers to join a grain storage and logistics operation in Nullawil, VIC, supporting the agriculture and grain industry.
This is a physically active, outdoor role where you will assist with site preparation ahead of harvest, including bunker work, grain handling, and receivals. You'll be working in a fast-paced environment alongside a team focused on safety, efficiency, and maintaining high site standards.
Key Responsibilities
This role supports daily grain site operations to ensure efficient handling, storage, and dispatch of grain.
Clean and prepare bunkers, including tarping and site setup
Shovel and move grain as required across the site
Assist with grain outloading and general site operations
Operate drive-over hoppers and support receivals
Conduct grain sampling and assist with weighbridge tasks
Follow directions from site management and maintain safe work practices
Benefits
Competitive pay rate of $33.38 per hour + penalty rates
Great working schedule of approximately 50 hours per week
Opportunity to gain hands-on experience across grain operations
Work outdoors as part of a supportive and safety-focused team
Variety of duties across receivals, bunker work, and grain handling
Your Skills & Experience
To be successful in this role, you will have previous experience in grain handling and be comfortable working in physically demanding environments.
Previous grain industry experience is essential
Ability to perform manual tasks including shovelling and bunker work
Comfortable working outdoors in varying weather conditions
Strong awareness of safety and site procedures
Reliable, punctual, and able to follow instructions

title: Victoria (VIC)
description:
16 mars
Hop Harvest in Myrtleford Victoria – immediate start!
Hop Harvest in Myrtleford Victoria – immediate start!
We’re looking for casual harvest workers for an immediate start. Harvest work is expected to go to the end of March.
Hops Products Australia (HPA) own and operate two hop farms in North East Victoria; Rostrevor Hop Gardens and Buffalo River Valley, both located a 10-minute drive from Myrtleford.
Very limited accommodation is available on site, so it is preferred that your have your own accommodation.
We need:
Casual harvest farm hands who will conduct a variety of tasks including
Tractor & Ute driving - transporting hops from the field to the processing facility, candidates must be licenced to drive a manual vehicle.
Truck driving- If applicant holds a minimum of an MC licence.
Vine Loading - unloading trailers of harvested hops, loading vines for picking and sweeping in our picking facility.
Forklift operating - if applicant holds an Australian forklift high risk work licence.
Hours
You’ll be required to work between 9 – 11.5 hour shifts depending on your role, up to 6 days per week for the duration of harvest. Day and night shifts are available.
Pay
All HPA harvest casuals are paid under the Horticultural Award at Level 3 $32.04. You will receive on the job role-specific training to ensure you are fully supported in your role.
You must be physically capable of safely performing manual handling tasks. You will need to have your own reliable transport to get to and from work.
Very limited on-site accommodation is available. It's preferred that you have you own.
TO APPLY
For further information or to apply email rhg@hops.com.au or contact the office on 03 5752 1701.

We are seeking Forklift Operators to join a busy almond processing facility in the agriculture and food processing industry. Based in Carwarp, VIC, this is a hands-on role within a fast-paced factory environment. You will be working as part of a supportive team, contributing to efficient processing and warehouse operations during the peak season.
Positions are available across two departments:
Inshell Forklift Operator - Responsible for accurately capturing pallet label information and recording it clearly. This role also involves stocking the pallet room and changing hooder film as required.

Operating forklifts safely and efficiently within the facility

title: Victoria (VIC)
description:
13 mars
Farm Hand - Carisbrook VIC
Farm Hand – Carisbrook VIC
About Pace
At Pace Farm, we’re proud of our strong family values that have guided us since the 1970s. From humble beginnings, we've grown into one of Australia’s largest egg producers, delivering the freshest, tastiest eggs nationwide. Our vision is simple yet powerful: Eggs for Everyone – ensuring that all Australians have access to nutritious, high-quality eggs every day. As the leading supplier of farm-fresh eggs and egg products, our mission is to continue setting the standard in the industry by valuing our people and equipping them with the skills to deliver top-quality products and service. With egg-citing growth and expansion on the horizon, now’s a great time to join us and be part of our continued success!
About the Role
Do you enjoy working with animals, being outdoors, and playing a hands-on role in a farming environment? We’re looking for a reliable and motivated Farm Hand to join our dedicated team in Carisbrook VIC.
Reporting to the Farm Supervisor, you’ll be responsible for supporting the daily operations of our farm — ensuring our birds are healthy, comfortable, and cared for to the highest standard. This is a great opportunity for someone looking for a stable and long-term role within a supportive and growing business.
What You’ll Do
Monitor bird feed and water supplies
Maintain high standards of shed cleanliness, hygiene, and biosecurity
Participate in continuous improvement and safety initiatives
Assist with general farm duties, including manual handling tasks
Support the Farm Supervisor and Manager with daily operations and reporting
Be proactive in improving safe work practices and the workplace environment
About You
You take pride in your work, have a strong work ethic, and enjoy working as part of a team. You don’t mind getting your hands dirty and thrive in a fast-paced, hands-on environment.
We’re looking for someone who is:
Reliable, hardworking, and safety-focused
A team player with a positive, can-do attitude
Physically fit and comfortable working in dusty environments (PPE provided)
Experienced in farming, animal handling, or shed work (highly regarded)
Flexible with working hours – you will be working on 4 days on, 4 days off roster 7am - 5.00pm. Therefore, weekend work will be required.
Why Join Pace Farm?
Training and career progression opportunities
Supportive and inclusive work culture
Free products and employee discounts on groceries, fuel, tech, fashion & more
Commitment to safety, wellbeing, and sustainability

title: Victoria (VIC)
description:
5 mars
Olive farm worker - Telopea Downs, Victoria
We are an olive and olive oil company seeking a causal farm worker for our orchard in Telopea Downs, Victoria. The job entails varied farm work including tractor work, managing trees (pruning, staking, tying etc) and assisting with the olive oil harvest, which begins in May. Due to the remote location, free accommodation is available but applicants must have their own vehicle.
As the job is physically demanding applicants should be fit, strong and have a good work ethic. Applicants with tractor experience are encouraged to apply.
The job starts immediately and the work for the right candidates, the work will be ongoing. We will have a heavy demand on staff during the harvest which we expect to run for 4-6 weeks between May and June.
The pay is the horticultural award rate for level 2, $31.19 per hour and $43.66 per hour for overtime hours. Most weeks there are at least 38 hours of work, with more during busy period, particularly during the harvest.
If you are interested, please email your resume including up to date references to neil.symington@oasisolives.com .

title: Victoria (VIC)
description:
23 fév.
Barn Animal Attendants - Casual (Burwood East)
About RSPCA Victoria
RSPCA Victoria is a non-government, community-based, not-for-profit organisation whose vision is ‘ending cruelty to all animals’. We are Victoria’s best-known and most respected animal welfare organisation forming part of the RSPCA Federation, with over 400 employees and 1400 volunteers in Victoria.
Our organisation’s purpose is with the community to achieve outstanding animal welfare through education, advocacy, and animal care and protection. RSPCA Victoria’s values of Clarity, Accountability, Respect, Consideration, Expertise and Collaboration – set clear expectations for all our staff and volunteers as they work together, and with the community.
About the role
The Animal Attendant is responsible for the provision of day to day care of the Education and Training/Barn animals (livestock, horses, small animals and poultry), and livestock and poultry in quarantine/adoption, contributing to the achievement of the best possible welfare outcome for every animal in our care.
Key responsibilities
In this rewarding role, you will enrich the lives of our furry residents, as well as:
Contribute to the daily management of the Barn animals (including livestock, horses, small animals and poultry), and livestock and poultry in quarantine/adoption, in a professional and caring manner.
Observe, document and report health and behaviour concerns of animals in our care, and support Veterinarians when required.
Assist the Education Team with school tours & school holiday programs, when required.
Provide a high level of service to customers in the adoption, surrender and reclaiming of livestock animals
Ensure all necessary documentation relating to the management of animals is completed accurately and in accordance with RSPCA Victoria procedures and timeframes.
What we’re looking for
Previous experience in an animal care role is required, including basic knowledge of animal anatomy, behaviour, disease recognition and safe handling;
Ability to work in a fast paced and challenging work environment, whilst showing great compassion to the animals in our care;
Demonstrated experience working collaboratively in a team environment;
Must be available to work weekends and public holidays.
Highly desirable
Demonstrated composure and expertise in working alongside livestock animals
What we can offer you
Access to a range of mental health and wellbeing supportive initiatives, including confidential counselling services and education programs.
Generous discount on products and services, including Royal Canin pet food, Hush Puppies footwear and RSPCA Victoria retail and vet clinic products / services.
Access to a range of learning and development opportunities.
Recruitment requirements
Valid and current driver's licence
Current Employee Working With Children's Check (or willingess to obtain one)
Current tetnaus vaccination (or willingness to obtain one)
How to Apply
To learn more about our organisation and this job opportunity, please visit our website at www.rspcavic.org and download the Position Description for more details. If you have any questions, please contact our Recruitment Team via recruitment@rspcavic.org.au for a confidential discussion.
Please note that we are actively reviewing applications and to apply as soon as possible

Our operations are in Western Australia, Victoria, and Tasmania, where we produce premium quality pork, beef, lamb, abalone, protein meal and tallow for clients and suppliers here locally and internationally. title: Victoria (VIC)
description:
19 fév.
Field Technician - East Coast, VIC
The Role
Join the team as a Field Technician supporting soil sampling and mapping operations across broadacre farms on the East Coast. Working with an innovative agribusiness leader, you'll be part of a practical, hands-on field team committed to delivering accurate data and supporting sustainable farming decisions. The work is physical, outdoors and varied - ideal for those who enjoy travel and working independently in rural environments.
Key Responsibilities
This role is focused on carrying out soil and data collection tasks safely, efficiently and in line with company standards. You will represent the business professionally while travelling between farm sites.
Key duties include:
Conducting field sampling and data collection activities following company protocols
Preparing and completing job packs, checklists and reporting procedures
Performing deep-core and shallow soil sampling, grid-based sampling, and EM38 and elevation mapping
Ensuring equipment is cleaned and maintained to meet biosecurity and safety requirements

Farm Hand / Stock Person
Farm Hand / Stockperson
Western Plains Pork – Near Ballarat, Victoria
The Company
Western Plains Pork is a leading free-range pig farming business located near Ballarat, Victoria. For more than 25 years, we've been producing high-welfare, premium pork for customers across Australia.
Our reputation is built on animal care, consistency, and doing things properly. We're a practical, progressive team who believe in our values – Honesty, Responsibility, Respect, and Resourcefulness – and we're now looking for the right people to join us on the farm.
The Role
We're seeking enthusiastic, reliable, and hands-on team players to join our operations team as Farm Hands / Stockpeople.
This is a practical, outdoor role working directly with livestock and supporting the daily running of our free-range pig operations.
No prior pig experience is required – full training will be provided for the successful applicants. What matters most is a positive attitude, willingness to learn, and a genuine care for animals.
You'll be involved in:
Operating tractors and towing various equipment
Performing daily checks on machinery and equipment
Assisting with stock movements, sorting, and weighing
Supporting feed and water checks, bedding, and general husbandry
Helping with fencing, weed spraying, and general farm maintenance
Following safe work and biosecurity procedures
Skills and Experience
Previous farm or animal experience preferred (but not essential)
Machinery operation skills an advantage
Physically fit and able to perform repetitive manual work
Reliable, proactive, and willing to learn new skills
Team player with good communication and problem-solving skills
Must hold a current car licence for transportation and equipment operation
The Right Person
Enjoys working outdoors in all weather conditions
Has a strong work ethic and pride in quality work
Values teamwork and respect
Brings a "can do, will do" attitude every day
Takes responsibility and looks for ways to improve
What's in it for You
Job security with a long-term career pathway
Ongoing training and skill development
Supportive team and management structure
Opportunities for additional hours and advancement
Company-supplied and laundered uniforms
Discounted pork products
Employee Assistance Program (EAP) – access to free counselling and life coaching
Be part of a company that genuinely promotes a Safety-First culture
What's Next
If this sounds like you, we'd love to hear from you.
Please email hr@westernplainspork.com, send your resume and tell us why you'd make a great addition to the Western Plains Pork team. All enquiries will be treated in confidence.

Are you enthusiastic about trees and dedicated to maintaining their health and beauty? Look no further!
The Advanced Tree Company, a reputable wholesale tree nursery located in Mornington, is seeking enthusiastic individuals to join our team. We are currently offering both full-time and casual positions with flexible hours that accommodate school schedules.

title: Victoria (VIC)
description:
5 fév.
Stable Hand
THE COMPANY
We are a boutique thoroughbred pre-training and spelling business located in Tuerong, on the Mornington Peninsula. We offer a range of pre-training, starting, spelling and rehabilitation services to a small handful of trainers, who prefer a highly personalised level of care for their thoroughbreds.
Located on one of Victoria's premier thoroughbred properties, we have a state of the art facility that spans across 70 acres of tree lined and picturesque land, coupled with a 1400m grass track, 1750m sand track, sand arena, 100 stables, a horse pool and 2 treadmills.
We are seeking additional team members so we can continue to provide the 5-star care our clients expect.
THE ROLE
With approx. 70 horses, the primary purpose of your role is to assist with the day-to-day care, exercise and welfare of the horses in our care.
Key responsibilities will include:
Handling horses of various ages
Feeding, grooming, rugging and mucking boxes
Tacking up / down of horses after exercise
Assisting farriers and vets, maintain animal health records and treating minor injuries
General upkeep and maintenance of the property general farm cleaning and tidying
WHAT'S OF OFFER:
If you love working in the racing industry but are exhausted by the 4:00am starts and would like a role with much better working conditions, then look no further…
We are able to offer a range of flexibility and roster options to suit your lifestyle, including:
Morning shift 6:00am to 12:00pm
Afternoon shift 2:00pm to 4:00pm
Up to 38 hours each week
Full time, Part time or Casual
No onsite accomodation available at present, but this could be a possibility in the future
You will also enjoy working alongside a great group of people, we value teamwork, sharing our horse knowledge and genuinely enjoy working together to ensure our horses receive 5-star care.
WHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR?
Candidates must have at least 2 years of horse handling experience with any type of horses.
Those with a basic knowledge of horse health care, common diseases and first aid including bandaging, wound care, administering medications will be highly regarded.
Additionally, you must also be:
Physically fit
Able to follow and carry out instructions
Self-motivated, observant and proactive
If you are undertaking any horse related studies, are an aspiring trainer or an equestrian looking to give the racing industry a go, please don't hesitate to get in touch.
Backpackers and other visa holders with full time working rights are welcome to apply. You must be located in Australia.
PLEASE NOTE: Our location does not qualify for the 88 day work/holiday visa extension
MORE INFORMATION
Please email resumes to stuart@racingpartners.com.au or for more information please call 0418 593 459.

title: South Australia (SA)
description:
19 mars
Entry Level Construction Position - NO EXPERIENCE REQUIRED
If you want to get into construction and you’re prepared to work for it, this is your opportunity.
We don’t expect experience. We expect effort.
Old Red Brick Company is hiring full-time labourers to join our demolition crew working across residential and commercial sites in Adelaide.
You will start at the bottom and earn your progression. Those who prove themselves will have opportunities to learn new skills and move into equipment operation.
🔧 What You’ll Be Doing
Assisting with strip-outs and demolition
Using hand and basic power tools
Site clean-up and material removal
Supporting operators and trades
Physical, hands-on work
Opportunity to learn to operate plant and equipment
This is real construction work — not light duties.
💪 What We’re Looking For
We want people who are:
Honest
Hardworking
Gritty and determined
Willing to learn
Team players
And importantly:
People who think, pay attention, and take responsibility for their actions.
Construction isn’t just physical — it requires awareness, common sense, and accountability.
You must understand that progression is earned. You start at the bottom and work your way up.
This is an excellent opportunity for someone who wants to grow, learn new skills, and become part of a family-run company.
✅ Requirements
Full, unrestricted working rights in Australia (non-negotiable). This means persons on student visa's are ineligible.
White Card
Driver’s licence preferred
Physically fit
Able to pass a medical
Police clearance (or willing to obtain one)
Willing to work around asbestos if required
Full-time, Monday–Friday.
📩 How to Apply
To apply, you must:
Email your resume, and
Include a 300-word statement titled “RED RABBIT”
Email: info@oldredbrickco.com.au
In your statement, explain:
Why you want the job
Why you’re the right fit
What work ethic means to you
This is your first opportunity to demonstrate that you have read the advertisement carefully, have attention to detail, and can follow instructions.
These are critical skills we are looking for.
Applicants who cannot demonstrate these skills will not be considered.
We are building careers — not filling short-term positions.

title: South Australia (SA)
description:
18 mars
Vintage Cellar Hands
The Company
Kingston Estate is one of Australia’s largest and most progressive family-owned wine companies and vineyard owners, having achieved award winning international recognition and success. Kingston Estate comprises of our winery based in the Riverland, office in Adelaide and 14 vineyard sites across South Australia and Victoria.
Our Vision: To be the Leading Grower and Producer of Australian Wine for the World.
The Role
Cellar Hands perform various tasks that contribute to the production and dispatch of wine.
This position will suit you if you are available for an immediate start, enjoy working in a team along with being outdoors and physically active.
Typical duties of this role include:
· Operation of equipment to crush and press grapes.
· Transfer operations.
· Machine and pump operations.
· Following and assisting with the review of Company Standard Operating Procedures.
· Performing tasks safely and within Company WHS policies and procedures.
· Data entry and auditing.
· Meeting our company Zero Loss guidelines.
· Maintaining equipment to a high standard.
· Other general responsibilities as required.
Requirements of the role:
· Be available for an immediate start.
· Be available to undertake rotational shift work, overtime and weekend work.
· Ability to keep accurate records and demonstrate excellent time management.
· Effective problem-solving skills and initiative.
· Ability to follow safe work procedures and contribute to as required.
· Be physically fit.
· A HR or Forklift licence would be considered an advantage.
· Current Confined Space training would be considered an advantage.
Benefits:
· Great team working environment.
· Opportunity to return each season.
· Invitation to employee recognition events.
· Competitive industry wages.
· Employee Assistance Program.
Working Holiday Makers and Accommodation Details:
Immediate start for experienced Winery workers and possibility of longer-term employment.
We do not provide accommodation, so all applicants must be willing to relocate to the Riverland and have suitable transport to commute to and from work, as no public transport is available in the area.
The successful candidate will share the values of our Company, Accountability, Teamwork, Continuous Improvement, Integrity and Respect.
How to Apply
If this position sounds like the right fit for you please apply, ensuring you attach both a resume and cover letter. For a confidential discussion about this position, please contact Human Resources on 08 8583 0500 or email hr@kewines.com.au.
Kingston Estate is an Equal Opportunity Employer and drug and alcohol-free workplace. All applicants are encouraged to apply and may be required to submit to pre-employment drug and alcohol testing as part of the recruitment process. It is our policy that we only respond to preferred candidates. Whilst we acknowledge your application, we may not communicate further with you.

title: South Australia (SA)
description:
18 mars
Farm Hand
Love being outdoors? Have a passion for Horticulture? Due to continued growth and the upcoming harvesting season, we are seeking a reliable and enthusiastic Farm Hand to join the Tony's Flowers team in Lenswood.
The Position
Working at our Lenswood Farm you will be responsible for general planting and harvesting activities including:
Ensuring optimal picking of flowers and plants daily,
Assisting with ground prep and crop planting,
Tending to pruning and general maintenance of crops,
Assisting with irrigation, spraying and fertigation; and
Operating general farm equipment in a safe manner including the use of farm implements, gators and irrigation equipment.
About Tony’s Flowers
Tony’s Flowers is South Australia’s leading wholesale supplier of locally grown and imported flowers. Our farm, located at Lenswood in the Adelaide Hills, plays a critical role ensuring that the highest quality flowers reach our key customers, from retailers & florists to independent grocers.
Your Skills and Experience
Previous experience working as a farm hand or in a nursery setting.
Can do ‘Hands On’ approach to work.
Good communication skills.
Ability to complete work in a safe manner, taking care to ensure own and others safety.
Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
Knowledge of, or developing knowledge of general horticulture/agriculture practices.
What’s Required
Drivers Licence (ability to travel to the location of the farm in Lenswood, Adelaide Hills).
Flexibility to work in all weather conditions, including high daytime temperatures of 35 degrees and periods of wind and rain.
Tony’s Flowers may require a pre-employment medical assessment and/or testing to ensure your suitability for the position and work environment, dependent on the nature of the position applying for.
Unrestricted work rights.
Benefits
Casual hourly rate, $30-$32/ hour.
Opportunity to convert to permanent staff for the right candidate.
Monday to Friday roster, with shifts between 7am-3pm.
Friendly and supportive team environment.
If this sounds like the role for you, email your resume to sales@tonysflowers.com.au

title: South Australia (SA)
description:
7 mars
Discovery Resorts Food and Beverage Attendant in Adelaide convention centre
About Us
G’day Group comprises leading Australian tourism brands in Discovery Parks, Resorts and the loyalty program G'day Rewards. Employing more than 2000 people Australia-wide, G’day Group has the largest park network in the country, offering an amazing customer journey.
Discovery Resorts - Wilpena, the gateway to the breathtaking Flinders Ranges, 430 kilometers north of Adelaide. Experience the beauty of the 800 million-year-old natural amphitheatre of Wilpena Pound and share in unforgettable adventures like scenic flights, 4WD tours, mountain biking, bushwalking, and Aboriginal cultural experiences. As the only accommodation in the Ikara-Flinders Ranges National Park, our resort offers a variety of stunning options, from glamping tents to modern resort rooms and campsites. Enjoy delicious local cuisine, exceptional stargazing, and become part of a team that immerses visitors in Australia’s stunning landscapes!
We strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. Wilpena Resort recognises their commitment to traditional owners of the land in which we operate, and therefore, encourage applications from Adnyamathanha peoples.
About the Role
As a Food & Beverage Attendant, you’ll be part of a friendly, guest-focused team delivering warm, efficient service across our food and beverage outlets. This is a hands-on role where no two days are the same — welcoming guests, serving food and drinks, and helping create memorable moments throughout their stay.
You’ll work closely with Supervisors, Managers and fellow team members to ensure service standards are met while maintaining a positive, inclusive and respectful workplace.
What You’ll Be Doing
Taking food and beverage orders and delivering friendly, efficient service
Clearing tables, resetting dining areas and maintaining a clean workspace
Responding to guest enquiries and resolving issues with professionalism
Following food safety, hygiene and workplace safety standards
Assisting with opening and closing duties as required
Working collaboratively with the wider resort team to support guest experiences
About You
A positive, reliable attitude and strong customer service skills
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, guest-facing environment
Willingness to learn — no prior experience required, full training provided
Able to work across a rotating 7-day roster
Pride in presentation, cleanliness and teamwork
A genuine interest in working respectfully on Country
What We Offer
Casual Rate: $32.31 p/h
Full uniform provided
Training, mentoring and ongoing support
Accommodation and meals at great rates
Join a fun, talented team in this stunning location
Discounted accommodation at over 300 G'day Group holiday parks nationally
Discounts with our G’day Rewards Partners
Service recognition awards
Access to Employee Assistance Program
Internal promotions and opportunities

title: South Australia (SA)
description:
12 fév.
Vineyard Operator - Harvest Casual
Company Description
We’re 30 years young but we have more than 130 years of combined experience. The Dorrien Estate Winery is nestled in the heart of the Barossa Valley and is steeped in tradition and rich culture. We have a diverse team of passionate growers and winemakers, including some 5th generation producers. This combined experience means we know a thing or two about turning high quality grapes into wine you want to drink!
With premium fruit from the best regions across the country, coupled with the expertise and experience of our team, every bottle of Dorrien Estate is crafted to bring you the best of modern Australian winemaking.
Dorrien Estate Winery is one of Australia’s leading grape crushing and Winemaking facilities. With two sites located in the heart of the Barossa Valley, the winery also provides full winemaking and processing services to contract customers.
Job Description
As a Vineyard Operator, you will be responsible for day-to-day vineyard operations across the Dorrien Estate sites for the duration of Vintage
You will work closely with the Vineyard Manager and other operators, assisting with vineyard operations.
What you'll do:
All Tractor operations, including spraying, mowing, canopy management and harvest activities.
Daily equipment cleaning, routine and preventative maintenance
Compliance and record keeping
Maintenance of vineyard sites, including surrounds, facilities, and reserves
Active OH&S and continuous improvement participation through safety meetings, toolboxes, and inspections
Qualifications
Now let’s talk about you:
Must be fully available for the duration of Vintage (Feb - May)
Previous experience in vineyard operations, or related agricultural experience
Tractor driving experience
Current Forklift License is essential
Flexibility to adapt to different work environments and vineyard sites
Willing to work shift-work, long hours and weekends for the duration of vintage.
Can work autonomously and problem solve as required
Able to safely lift and carry heavy items, and drive for extended periods, including on rough terrain

Working in Citrus packing sheds

Piggery Attendant (Farm Hand) - Entry Level
Who We Are
SunPork Group is Australia’s leading farmer-owned and operated pork producer, committed to delivering the highest quality fresh Australian pork to market. With over 1,800 employees across 52 locations in Australia and New Zealand, we take pride in being a trusted name in the industry.
We’re passionate about what we do through our commitment to growth, to our people and to the responsible and ethical care of our animals—ensuring their wellbeing, health and comfort, is at the heart of everything we do.
In joining us, you will become part of an ever-growing business – passionate about producing the best quality Aussie pork for Aussie plates – from farm to plate.
Opportunity
We’re looking for an enthusiastic Piggery Attendant to join our farming team. This is an entry-level position, perfect for someone wanting to build a career in agriculture. You’ll work alongside experienced farm staff, learning all aspects of pig care, production, and animal welfare, with the chance to progress and develop your career in livestock production.
Reporting to the Farm Manager, the purpose of this role is to be a key player in keeping our pigs healthy, happy and well cared for.
SunPork Company Benefits:
As a SunPork Employee you will have access to the below:
The opportunity to begin your career in agriculture or livestock production, with full training provided to support growth and development
The chance to earn a Certificate III in Pork Production
Discounts at over 400 retailers - including big name brands
Become a part of a supportive and down-to-earth farm team
About the Role
As a key player in the day-to-day operations of our farm, you'll be responsible for:
Feeding, monitoring and caring for our pigs - ensuring top-notch animal health and welfare
Carrying out health checks, administering treatments and following vaccination programs
Maintaining clean pens, equipment and facilities to the highest standards
Record animal health and production data accurately
Learn and apply biosecurity and safety practices
About You
Our ideal candidate will have the following:
Animal experience? Great! But not essential, we'll train you
A strong commitment to animal welfare and biosecurity
A positive can-do attitude that fits our core values of trust, integrity, quality, efficiency and innovation
Availability to work weekends and public holidays when needed
Physical fitness and comfortable with hands-on farm work
SunPork is an equal opportunity employer. Applicants must have the right to work in Australia.

Warehouse/Delivery & Sales Assistance
Warehouse, Delivery & Sales Assistant
Flex Fitness Equipment – Australia’s Fastest Growing Fitness Retailer
About Us
Flex Fitness Equipment is one of Australia’s leading specialty fitness retailers with 7 busy stores nationwide and a rapidly growing online presence.
Our team is made up of genuine fitness enthusiasts who love helping customers build their dream gyms.
We’re expanding fast and need energetic, positive people to join our crew.
The Role – Warehouse, Delivery & Sales Assistant (Casual 2-3 days)
A position is available in our new Adelaide store.
This is a true hybrid role, perfect for someone who is happy to roll up their sleeves in the warehouse and loves being on the shop floor talking fitness if needed.
You’ll split your time roughly 80/20 between warehouse,deliveries and sales (depending on store needs).
What You’ll Be Doing
Warehouse, Delivery and Assembly Duties
Receiving and checking in deliveries
Picking and packing online orders accurately and quickly
Stock replenishment and rotation
Loading/unloading customer vehicles (ute/trailer loading is a daily thing)
General warehouse maintenance and organisation
Occasional local deliveries, assemblies or store transfers (company vehicle provided)
Sales and store Duties
Greeting and assisting walk-in customers
Demonstrating equipment, answering technical questions, and helping customers choose the right gear for their goals
Processing sales, phone/email enquiries
Keeping the showroom looking sharp and fully stocked
What We’re Looking For
Passion for fitness and training — you don’t need to be a bodybuilder or CrossFit games athlete, but you must genuinely love the gym and understand equipment
Warehouse/stock handling experience is a bonus
Physically fit and capable of regular heavy lifting (up to 32kg plates, bumpers, rigs, etc.)
Previous retail sales or customer service experience (fitness industry experience highly regarded but not essential — we’ll teach you the products)
Great communication skills and a confident, friendly personality
Reliable, punctual, and thrives in a fast-paced environment
Full Australian working rights
Current driver’s licence (manual preferred but not essential)
Forklift licence (advantage but we can help you get one)
What We Offer
Casual role (2-3 days a week)
Fun, supportive team culture, we train together, spot each other, and celebrate wins
Uniform provided

Why you want to work at Flex Fitness Equipment

The Company
Kingston Estate is one of Australia’s largest and most progressive family-owned wine companies and vineyard owners, having achieved award winning international recognition and success. Kingston Estate comprises of our winery based in the Riverland, office in Adelaide and 14 vineyard sites across South Australia and Victoria.
Our Vision: To be the Leading Grower and Producer of Australian Wine for the World.
The Role
Cellar Hands perform various tasks that contribute to the production and dispatch of wine.
This position will suit you if you enjoy working in a team along with being outdoors and physically active.
Typical duties of this role include:
·         Operation of equipment to crush and press grapes.
·         Transfer operations.
·         Machine and pump operations.
·         Following and assisting with the review of Company Standard Operating Procedures.
·         Performing tasks safely and within Company WHS policies and procedures.
·         Data entry and auditing.
·         Meeting our company Zero Loss guidelines.
·         Maintaining equipment to a high standard.
·         Other general responsibilities as required.
Requirements of the role:
·         Be available to work from Monday 3 February – Friday 24 April 2026.
·         Be available to undertake rotational shift work, overtime and weekend work.
·         Ability to keep accurate records and demonstrate excellent time management.
·         Effective problem-solving skills and initiative.
·         Ability to follow safe work procedures and contribute to as required.
·         Be physically fit.
·         A HR or Forklift licence would be considered an advantage.
·         Current Confined Space training would be considered an advantage.
Benefits:
·         Great team working environment.
·         Opportunity to return each season.
·         Invitation to employee recognition events.
·         Competitive industry wages.
·         Employee Assistance Program.

description:

https://4gexpo.com.au/working-with-us/
Event Setup and Pack down Crew Member
Job Overview: Are you looking for a hands-on role in a dynamic, fast-paced environment? We’re seeking motivated individuals to join our team for event and exhibition setup and pack down. You’ll play an essential role in bringing events to life—from large-scale productions like the ADL 500/Liv golf to smaller exhibitions and festive activations, such as Christmas-themed displays. This is a great opportunity for anyone who enjoys working behind the scenes in the event industry, ensuring that each experience is flawlessly executed.
Position Details:
Duration: Looking for crew members for work commencing asap with the potential for ongoing opportunities based on performance and event demand.
Hours: Shifts will vary, with weekly hours ranging from 10 to 50 hours, depending on event schedules. As we operate 7 days a week, flexibility is essential. We aim to accommodate your availability, so you’re encouraged to specify the days and times that suit your schedule.
Location: You’ll be working across various event locations, so a willingness to travel within the city is necessary.
What You’ll Be Doing: Assisting in the setup of event structures, exhibits, displays, and decorations, ensuring everything is in place before the event starts. Packing down and dismantling all equipment and structures after events, ensuring everything is carefully stored or transported as required. Working as part of a close-knit team to deliver high-quality event production, often under time constraints. Liaising with event coordinators and other crew members to ensure smooth operations. Adhering to safety protocols and ensuring that all equipment is handled carefully to avoid damage.
Why Work With Us: Gain hands-on experience in the exciting event and exhibition industry. Be part of a supportive and collaborative team environment. Flexible hours that allow you to work around other commitments. Potential for ongoing work and career development opportunities for the right candidates. Opportunity to be involved in some of the most exciting and high-profile events in the region.
Requirements:
A reliable means of transport, whether it’s your own vehicle or easy access to public transportation, as events may take place at various locations.
Strong English language skills, both written and verbal, to ensure clear communication with the team and event coordinators.
A professional appearance and a positive attitude—first impressions matter in the events industry!
White Card, is required for most sites that we work on.
A keen eye for detail and the ability to work efficiently and responsibly, even under pressure.
Previous experience in events, exhibitions, or manual labor roles is a plus but not essential. We value a strong work ethic and a willingness to learn.
This is a physically demanding role that requires lifting, moving, and setting up equipment outside in the weather. A good level of physical fitness is essential.
How to Apply: If you’re ready to be part of an exciting industry and contribute to memorable events, we want to hear from you! Please send your resume along with your availability and any relevant experience

title: South Australia (SA)
description:
19 sept. 2025
SOUTH AUSTRALIA - GRAIN HARVEST JOBS
Looking for a seasonal job this summer? Join PROGRAMMED working the Harvest!
Make new friends, learn new skills, gain new experiences, and earn some money!
Join PROGRAMMED for an Exciting Summer Harvest!
Looking for a seasonal job this summer? Join us for an extraordinary harvest experience at one of Cargill's South Australian sites.
Empowering Global Agriculture: Cargill, the world's largest Agriculture Company, creates vital connections across the Australian and global food system, nourishing the world. As a Harvest casual, you'll play a crucial role in GrainFlow operations, facilitating grain receival and storage through a network of 15 storage centres across four states.
Diverse Roles, No Experience Required: Whether you're an experienced agriculture worker or a first timer, we have a place for you in our dynamic harvest teams as most seasonal harvest roles require no prior experience. We seek dedicated, hardworking individuals who are reliable team players with a willingness to learn.
Experience Regional Australia: Immerse yourself in the essence of Australian rural life with our exciting opportunities in scenic regional locations across Southern Queensland, New South Wales, Victoria, and South Australia. Share this extraordinary experience with family, friends, or partners.
We have the below roles currently available;
Locations;
Pinnaroo, SA
Mallala, SA
Maitland, SA
Crystal Brook, SA
Grain Samplers:
Sampling and testing the grain. Comprehensive training provided as Grain Samplers play a crucial role in maintaining the quality and integrity of grain stocks
Provide exceptional customer service to growers and suppliers
Collaborate with the logistics team to ensure smooth grain flow
Utilize data entry skills to accurately record sample details and results
Grain Handlers:
Receive full training to operate and maintain grain storage machinery
Safely load and unload grain from trucks and other equipment
Employ efficient shovelling, sweeping, and handling techniques to maximize productivity
Assist with grain transfer and blending operations
Participate in bulk storage tasks, including tarping and bunker work, to preserve grain quality
Weighbridge Operators:
Full training provided for efficient weighbridge operation and record-keeping
Greet and assist drivers entering and exiting the facility
Accurately weigh and record grain quantities for transactions
Ensure compliance with all safety and regulatory procedures
Provide excellent customer service and address inquiries professionally
Plant Operators:
Safely operate and maintain mobile equipment such as Front-End Loaders, Telehandlers, and Water Trucks
Support the loading and unloading of grain trucks and railcars
Assist with general site maintenance and cleaning activities
Work collaboratively with the team to achieve production targets
Prioritize safety at all times and operate machinery following stringent safety processes
Who we are
As one of Australia's largest employers, we work with great companies all around Australia and New Zealand to give you the best opportunities to work when and where you want to.
At Programmed, we are committed to creating a safe and inclusive environment which values and respects diverse styles, backgrounds, experiences and perspectives.
We welcome and encourage women, people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent and people from diverse backgrounds to apply.
So it's over to you. If you would like to join our team, APPLY now through SEEK.
https://www.seek.com.au/job/87128447?type=standard&ref=search-standalone#sol=285c8b862be1799e9a254ca6b62c9ae6bb34a864
To learn more about working with Programmed, we encourage you to visit our website.

Are you looking for a hands-on role in the beautiful Riverland region, where your work feeds families across the world while contributing to a sustainable future? Costa Group, Australia's largest grower, packer, and supplier of premium citrus and grapes, is seeking a dedicated and energetic Farm Hand to join our Riverland Citrus Farming operations.
Who We’re Looking For
The role of Farm Hand is perfect for those who thrive outdoors and enjoy hands-on work. Whether you’ve worked on farms before or are completely new to the industry, we’re looking for people with a positive, “can-do” attitude, a willingness to learn, and the availability to work a flexible roster to meet harvest and operational requirements. Reliability, a strong work ethic, and a focus on safety are key to succeeding in this role.
Applicants must have full and unrestricted work rights in Australia. Sponsorship is not available for this role.
About the Role
As a Farm Hand, you’ll play a vital role in our farming operations, with responsibilities including:
Operating tractor sprayer to perform weeding, slashing, mulching, citrus harvest/board casting fertiliser duties.
Basic service and maintenance of farm equipment.
Mixing and distribution of fertilisers, and accurate recording of fertiliser usage.

General farm labour as required.
You’ll work directly under the guidance of our Farm Manager, receiving on-the-job training and opportunities to grow and develop your skills and career. This is a great opportunity to immerse yourself in horticulture and be part of a team committed to sustainable farming and delivering premium quality fruit.

title: South Australia (SA)
description:
30 mai 2025
Production labourers on a mushroom farm in Woodcroft SA
Summary:
We’re a family-run mushroom farm with a passion for quality & teamwork. We’re looking for a fit and reliable production labourer with solid forklift experience, and ideally a MR (Medium Rigid) or higher truck license, to join our production team. This is a hands-on role requiring physical fitness and a strong work ethic. You’ll be working in a dirty, farm-based environment, so being comfortable in that setting is essential.
Our Family Farm:
We are a family-owned business since 1992, where White and Swiss Brown mushrooms are freshly handpicked. We produce around 45 Tonnes of mushrooms a week and supply all around Australia, including some of the major supermarkets, such as Woolworths, Drakes, Foodland & Coles. Within our farm we have 80 employees and we work within a multicultural environment. Creating a warm-hearted culture, where all of our employees are like family to us, we work with passion to produce the highest quality of mushrooms.
Key Duties include and are not limited to:
Operating a forklift and/or truck
Working along the production line
General cleaning
Watering and caring for produce
Repairing mushroom trays
What We’re Looking For:
Previous experience in a production/labourer role
Valid forklift license (essential)
MR truck license or above (preferred)
Be hard working, highly reliable and able to work independently
Willingness to work in a hands-on and sometimes messy environment
Shows initiative and a strong interest in long-term growth with us
Be in good physical health to meet the demands of the job
Work Hours:
Monday to Friday, between 6:00 AM and 5:00 PM
Occasional weekend work may be required
info@puremushrooms.com.au

title: South Australia (SA)
description:
11 fév. 2025
3 x Farm Workers needed with Experience
Bell Group Australia An opportunity for up to 3 young farmers to experience and work for a progressive and large scale broadacre cropping and livestock enterprise based in Australia. The company has two locations in both Millicent, South Australia and Narromine, New South Wales. Both properties are very diverse and farming practises differ in each location which will ad to the learnings and different experiences whilst working here. A big part of our success is the people within the business. During our busy periods there is up 18 team members who all care and support each other whilst achieving goals together. The Narromine aggregation consists of 4000ha of crop in central west region of New South Wales this rotation includes canola (rapeseed), lentils, chickpeas wheat and barley. Hay and straw is also apart of the northern operation as well as a cattle trading enterprise. The Millicent aggregation consists of 3000ha of crop in the high rainfall zone of Southern Australia. Crops grown are canola (rapeseed), wheat, broad beans, small seeds and hay. This is done in conjunction with a 12,000 head composite sheep breeding flock. The role would enable the successful applicants the chance to experience a full season including seeding and harvest across both Aggregations starting at Narromine in April and Finishing in February at Millicent. The roles can be tailored around holidaying/travel periods which is encouraged by our management whilst here in Australia. The successful applicant would posses a good attitude, willingness to learn and a passion for agriculture. They are required to be punctual, well mannered, work in an unsupervised capacity at times, have attention to detail, reporting to the manager on a regular basis. The different roles: -combine harvesting -Windrowing -chaserbin driving -tillage -air seeding -truck driving -hay duties such as mowing, raking, baling, stacking etc -general farm work and maintenance -various livestock work All machinery is modern and uses the latest in gps technology. The fleet is all modern John Deere fleet with X9 combines for 2025 harvest. Good Accomodation and meals are supplied during the busy periods. The Bell Group are involved in its nearby communities with opportunities to socialise outside of work at local sporting clubs and with neighbouring farmers at different events. This is an experience of a lifetime for those with the right attitude and willingness to take on the challenge. The fun along the way, knowledge gained, high level of achievement and lifelong friendships made makes working in such a operation a experience that one will never forget. Contact Tom on +61439118010 or email your resume to tom.bell@live.com.au

Solar Eggs is a South Australian business success story. Evolving from its humble beginning as a hobby farm in 1927, Solar Eggs is now positioned at the forefront of egg marketing, farming and supply in Australia. Significant and continuous investment into plant and equipment ensures Solar Eggs’ operation and quality assurance procedures exceed industry standards.
Solar Eggs brands include Barossa, Gourmet Breakfast, Adelaide Egg, Hens on the Range and Omega 3 Eggs. We currently supply over 1,000,000 eggs each week, retailing across South Australia in supermarkets and specialty stores.

We're now looking for an enthusiastic, reliable individual to join our growing team as Window & Carpet Cleaners, Tile & Grout Specialists, and Pressure Washing Experts.

Window Cleaning:
Clean windows to a streak-free shine for residential and commercial clients.

Koompartu Farms (Cooltong Station) is a newly developed large-scale almond property in Monash, South Australia. This year we completed planting trees in 1640 ha, in addition to the 860 ha planted last year to become one of the largest almond properties in Australia.
The role
We are looking for energetic, experienced, and safety-conscious Orchard Hands to join the irrigation team. You will carry out a range of orchard irrigation activities including repair and maintenance of irrigation systems, flushing filters, and checking irrigation lines. You may also be required to carry out operational activities such as operating farm machinery, spraying weedicides, herbicides and fungicides, and slashing weeds.
We will support you in building a long-term career with us and support your professional development through certifications, and industry/job specific training courses.

have at least one year of experience in a similar capacity with a good understanding of orchard activities (preferably in almonds) including irrigation
be able to safely operate a range of farm machinery
have a strong commitment to work health and safety
have a valid driver’s licence
have chemical handling certification (or willingness to obtain)
have a forklift licence (or willingness to obtain)
be willing to work flexible hours and work outdoors
have the physical capacity to carry out essential duties of the role
be able to work independently and in a team
have excellent interpersonal skills
To find out more about this opportunity, contact us on 0455 665 567.

title: South Australia (SA)
description:
20 déc. 2024
Vintage Winemaker in Adelaide hills
About the Role:
We are looking for a qualified Winemaker, who is quality focussed and has a strong passion for wine to join our small but highly skilled team during Vintage 2025.
You will work closely with our very knowledgeable and highly experienced Chief Winemaker, in a hands-on capacity in the winery, cellar and laboratory. You will be involved in all aspects of the winemaking process and focussed on maintaining quality control and product integrity to ensure that high quality wine is produced.
Key Responsibilities:
Production and processing of premium wines
Vintage processing and barrel management
Monitoring ferments/wines to ensure quality and within specifications set by Chief Winemaker
Supervise crushing of grapes
Creating accurate cellar notes in a timely manner
Preparation of wines for bottling/despatch
Staff supervision and management
Maintain and update winery records
About You:
The ideal candidate will need to be enthusiastic, reliable, detail orientated, able to work well under pressure, have excellent communication skills and be adaptable to changing priorities. You will also have strong planning, organisational, leadership, problem solving and decision-making skills; and a dedication to uphold quality and safe work practices.

title: Tasmania (TAS)
description:
18 mars
Casual Farmhand
About us
Pure Foods Eggs is a proud Tasmanian company that has been in operation for over 30 years, spanning egg production and wholesale distribution. Pure Foods Eggs is Tasmania’s premier egg producer, distributing over 4 million dozen eggs per year. We are passionate about great Tasmanian products, and we pride ourselves on delivering the highest quality produce. We supply to retailers including Coles, Woolworths and IGA, making sure that more Tasmanian’s wake up to a Pure Foods egg than any other brand. Pure Foods is committed to its local community and supports numerous not-for-profit organisations across the state.
Tasks & responsibilities
This role works closely with the senior management team and is a key contributor to the successful delivery of operational outcomes.
This is a Casual (approx 25-30hrs per week) position under the Pastoral Award. Work hours are generally 6am to 2pm, however, these hours can vary depending on the needs of the farm & may require some weekend work. The role is located in Longford.
Key responsibilities include:
Learning about the care of and maximising the welfare of layer hens
Monitoring and reporting on hen welfare
Routine inspection, cleaning and maintenance (sheds & yards)
Moving chickens from one area of the farm to another
Problem solving – identifying and fixing issues
Biosecurity – limiting the risk of the introduction of pests and diseases that could harm the hens
Collaborating with farm management to continually improve farm operations
Other tasks as directed
Benefits
Fantastic farm environment
Supportive team
Training and development opportunities
On-site parking
This is an exciting opportunity to join a successful Tasmanian company and be part of a diverse and dynamic team. If this sounds like you, we would love to hear from you!
Shortlisted applicants will be required to undertake a pre-employment medical so we can be sure you are physically able to do the tasks required in the role.
Applications close on Friday, April 10, 2026
admin@purefoods.com.au

Vineyard Hand
Vineyard Hand - Vintage 26
Commence Mid-March to Late April
Glenview, Dalrymple and Jansz Vineyards are currently recruiting a seasonal Vineyard hand with a focus on machinery operation in our picturesque White Hills and Pipers Brook Vineyards in Northern Tasmania.

Reporting to the Southern Vineyard Manager, this role involves the completion of all vineyard operations, ensuring that the tasks are completed safely to specification and within the required time frames
