title: Tasmania (TAS)
description:
25 fév.
Hatchery Attendant
Full-Time Hatchery Attendant – Cressy, Tasmania
Petuna
Petuna has grown from a successful fishing operation founded by Peter and Una Rockliff in 1949 into a leading Tasmanian aquaculture company. We grow, harvest, and process premium salmonids for wholesale and retail markets, with operations including a state-of-the-art hatchery in Cressy, marine farms in Macquarie Harbour and Rowella, and a cutting-edge production plant in Devonport.
At Petuna, our people are at the heart of what we do. Join a company that embraces innovation, sustainability, and a strong commitment to safety, wellbeing, and career development.
About the Role
We are seeking a full-time Hatchery Attendant to join our operations team at our Cressy Hatchery, located approximately 30 minutes from Launceston.
This is a hands-on role working across the early life stages of salmon and trout production, including juvenile fish rearing and broodstock care. You’ll be part of a small, dedicated team operating advanced Recirculating Aquaculture Systems (RAS) and flow-through systems in a technically sophisticated environment.
A range of roster options are available, including day shifts and shift work. Part-time and casual employment arrangements may also be considered.
This is an excellent opportunity to contribute to a high-performing aquaculture operation where teamwork, precision and fish welfare are critical to success.
Duties
Reporting to the Operations Manager, you will:
Follow all safety procedures and actively contribute to a strong safety culture.
Work collaboratively within a small, high-performing team.
Support fish health, husbandry and animal welfare in line with company policies and procedures.
Complete and accurately record feeding activities across RAS and flow-through systems.
Monitor and maintain optimal water quality parameters.
Operate and maintain hatchery equipment and systems.
Clean tanks, ponds and pump screens.
Support operation of the RAS wastewater treatment system.
Respond to alarms and after-hours issues (as trained), following established procedures.
Maintain strict biosecurity protocols to protect fish health and site integrity.
About You
We’re looking for someone who is reliable, practical and motivated, with a strong commitment to safety and quality.
You will ideally have:
Certificate III in Aquaculture (or relevant qualification) will be highly regarded.
Support to obtain a qualification may be considered for suitable candidates.
Previous experience in aquaculture, hatchery or farming environments will be highly regarded.
Strong technical aptitude and problem-solving skills.
A proactive, “can-do” attitude.
Commitment to maintaining a safe and compliant workplace.
Current right to work in Australia.
A valid driver’s licence.
Why Petuna
Stable, full-time employment in a technically advanced hatchery operation utilising modern Recirculating Aquaculture Systems (RAS).
Be home every night, no remote rosters or DIDO arrangements.
Supportive, team-orientated culture.
Opportunities for ongoing training and career development.
Job security with a respected Tasmanian employer.
Employee benefits:
Heavily discounted staff sales.
Wellbeing initiatives, including up to 4 EAP sessions per occasion (extended to immediate family).
Discounted private health insurance through St Luke’s Health.
If you’re ready to build a career in aquaculture and contribute to a team that values safety, teamwork and excellence, we encourage you to apply.
Please email your resumé and cover letter to careers@petuna.com.
Working visa holders must include current VEVO documentation.

Warehouse Operator - Casual
We have an exciting opportunity for a Warehouse Operator to join our high-performing and friendly team at our Distribution Centre in Devonport, TAS, employed on a casual basis with possible weekend work included. This is a casual position, but there are plenty of hours available.
ONLY THOSE WHO CAN START IMMEDIATELY NEED APPLY!
About Your Position:
You will play a key role in the overall success of our operations by helping ready quality fresh produce for distribution to stores across Tasmania. Duties include:
Picking accurate orders to customer requirements
Inventory management
Keeping the warehouse and surrounding areas are clean and safe to store fresh produce
Stack pallets so stock won't be damaged in loading or in transit
Working safely, always
Working in a refrigerated environment
Operating electric pallets jacks
Manual handling, with repetitive lifting of stock up to 20kg
About You:
Previous warehouse (or similar) experience preferable
Ability to work as part of a team
Good communication skills
Willing to work together to achieve results
Basic computer skills
Attention to detail whilst working in a fast pace environment
About Us:
Costa Group is Australia's leading grower, packer and marketer of fresh fruit and vegetables. Costa supplies quality fruits and vegetables to all the major Australia supermarket chains as well as independent grocers and food industry stakeholders.
Costa’s Wholesale and Logistics Division operates wholesale produce market sales, providoring and supply chain functions, with temperature-controlled facilities and operations in most states servicing the needs of our Costa Categories as well as retail, FMCG and foodservice customers.
We provide:
On-the job training and support
Positive and inclusive company culture
Plenty of hours
JOIN US TODAY! Must have current Right to Work in Australia with an immediate start. Apply now on our website.

Contract Milker or Lower Order Sharefarmer
Located at Parkham, Northwest Tasmania (10 minutes to Deloraine, 30 minutes from Devonport and 45 minutes to Launceston)
Commencing 1st July 2026 season
TasCor is seeking an experienced Contract Milker or Lower Order Sharefarmer for one of its dairy farms located at Parkham in Tasmania’s North West.
Farm details:
650 spring calving crossbred cows 36-aside herringbone dairy
145 ha under centre pivot irrigation (2 pivots)
The role involves responsibility for the day-to-day dairy operations including milking, pasture and feed management, young stock management, herd health, staff employment and supervision, irrigation oversight, record keeping and reporting.
This position would suit an experienced lower order sharefarmer, contract milker or a senior dairy operator ready to step into a contract role. The farm is located close to Deloraine, Launceston and Devonport with access to schools and essential services.
Remuneration: Is on a $/kg Milk solids or a percentage of the milk cheque in the case of the share farming agreement. House included.
To apply, please send a cover letter, resume and at least two referees to admin@tascoragri.com.
For further information call 0364 275310
Applications without a resume and references will not be considered.
*Must have full working rights in Australia

title: Tasmania (TAS)
description:
9 fév.
Farm Job - Great team - Northern Tasmania
Join a Dairy Team You’ll Be Proud to Be Part Of
Join our progressive dairy business milking approximately 2,000 cows across two farms in Northern Tasmania — an excellent opportunity for someone – or a couple - looking to build a long-term career in the industry.
We believe great farms are built by great teams. When you join us, you’re not just stepping into a role — you’re becoming part of a supportive, high-functioning group that communicates well, works together, and takes pride in what we achieve.
We are protective of our team culture and hire people who strengthen it. If you value respect, accountability, and teamwork, you’ll fit right in. Experience is welcome, but it isn’t essential. What matters most is your attitude and your ability to contribute positively to the team.
Why people enjoy working here:
✔ Strong teamwork — no egos, no passengers
✔ Support from experienced staff who genuinely want to see you succeed
✔ A safe, respectful workplace
✔ Opportunities to learn, develop skills, and build a real career in dairy
✔ Employers who recognise effort, initiative, and reliability
Our team enjoys coming to work, looks after one another, and puts animal wellbeing at the centre of everything we do.
The Opportunity
You’ll gain experience across all aspects of the farm, including:
• Milking and animal care
• General farm duties
• Maintaining high farm standards
• Working alongside a reliable and supportive crew
The Type of Person Who Thrives Here:
You don’t need to know everything, you don’t even need experience in dairy — but you do need to:
✔ Work well with others
✔ Communicate openly and respectfully
✔ Take responsibility for your role
✔ Show up with a positive attitude
✔ Be willing to learn and improve
Accommodation can be provided if required, although local applicants are encouraged to apply. These roles are not suitable for people seeking their 88 day VISA.
If you’re looking for more than “just a job” and want to belong to a team where you’ll be supported, challenged, and valued — we’d love to hear from you.
📩 Send us a message with a bit about yourself.
james@rosemountag.com

title: Tasmania (TAS)
description:
27 jan.
Hop Harvest Casual Seasonal Work TAS 2026
HOP HARVEST IN TASMANIA
We’re looking for casual seasonal workers for our 2026 hop harvest for the month of March 2026.
Our hop farm at Bushy Park is located in Southern Tasmania, a 20-minute drive from New Norfolk and a 50-minute drive from Hobart.
Limited on-site accommodation options including housing or camping is available.
A variety of Hop harvest roles include:
Tractor driving & Vine loading - transporting hops from the field to the processing facility, unloading the trailers of harvested hops, loading vines for picking
Sweeping in our processing facility
Picking Machine operators (basic mechanical aptitude is required)
Forklift operators (must have a current Australian forklift high risk work licence)
Laboratory and Quality control
You’ll be required to work between 9 – 11 hour shifts depending on your role, up to 6 days per week for the duration of harvest. Day and night shifts are available.
You will be paid a competitive casual hourly rate (between Level 3 $32.04 and Level 4 $33.19 per hour depending on your role) under the Horticultural Award, including paid induction and training. You will receive on the job role-specific training to ensure you are fully supported in your role.
You must be physically capable of safely performing manual handling tasks. You will need to have your own reliable transport to get to and from work.
TO APPLY
For further information or to apply contact the farm office on 03 62861329 or drop into the Bushy Park Estates farm office located at 440 Gordon River Road, Bushy Park.
We will contact you if your application is successful. Due to the large number of applications we receive we will be unable to contact all applicants individually.
Submitting an application does not guarantee employment at HPA. All applications will remain confidential and will not be used for marketing purposes.

We supply to retailers including Coles, Woolworths and IGA, making sure that more Tasmanian’s wake up to a Pure Foods egg than any other brand. Pure Foods is committed to its local community and supports numerous not-for-profit organisations across the state.
Tasks & responsibilities
This role works closely with the senior management team and is a key contributor to the successful delivery of operational outcomes.
This is a Casual (approx 25-30hrs per week) position under the Pastoral Award. Work hours are generally 6am to 2pm, however, these hours can vary depending on the needs of the farm & may require some weekend work. The role is located in Longford.
Key responsibilities include:
Learning about the care of and maximising the welfare of layer hens
Monitoring and reporting on hen welfare
Routine inspection, cleaning and maintenance (sheds & yards)
Moving chickens from one area of the farm to another
Problem solving – identifying and fixing issues
Biosecurity – limiting the risk of the introduction of pests and diseases that could harm the hens
Collaborating with farm management to continually improve farm operations
Other tasks as directed
Benefits
Fantastic farm environment
Supportive team
Training and development opportunities
On-site parking
This is an exciting opportunity to join a successful Tasmanian company and be part of a diverse and dynamic team. If this sounds like you, we would love to hear from you!
Shortlisted applicants will be required to undertake a pre-employment medical so we can be sure you are physically able to do the tasks required in the role.
Applications close on Friday, February 1, 2026
admin@purefoods.com.au

title: Tasmania (TAS)
description:
14 jan.
Processing Attendant - Margate Tasmania
At Tassal Group, we are dedicated to “Sustainably Feeding Tomorrow.”
With over 35 years of aquaculture experience, we focus on providing healthy, sustainable, and nutritious food, including salmon, prawn, and barramundi. We are committed to producing accessible food, supporting communities, and delivering strong economic results, all while prioritising sustainability to ensure a prosperous, healthy planet for future generations.
The role:
MULTIPLE CASUAL POSITIONS AVAILABLE | IMMEDIATE START
Join Tassal’s fast-paced salmon processing factory in Margate as a casual Processing Attendant. In this role, you’ll assist with the processing and production of fresh salmon, working on a production line and operating machinery to cut, weigh, and package a range of products.
This is an exciting opportunity to be part of Australia’s largest salmon producer, with on-the-job training provided. If you’re reliable, have a great attitude, and are ready to take on a new challenge, we’d love to hear from you!
Responsibilities:
Assist with the processing and production of fresh salmon on a production line.
Operate machinery to cut, weigh, and package a variety of products.
Maintain high standards of quality and efficiency in a fast-paced environment.
Follow safety and hygiene protocols to ensure a safe and compliant workplace.
Role requirements:
Full availability to work shifts Monday to Friday each week.
Immediate availability.
Ability to work in a cold food-processing environment.
Excellent communication skills.
Reliable transport to get to work (car and driver’s licence required).
Forklift ticket highly desirable.
Ability to pass a pre-employment medical assessment and drug test.
Full-time working rights in Australia.
Role offerings:
Hourly rate starting from $30 per hour (including 25% casual loading).
Work with a supportive, passionate, and collaborative team.
Huge potential for career development and progression.
Generous employee discount on our range of seafood products.
Access to Maxxia Salary Packaging, Novated Leasing & Rewards programme.
Workplace health and wellbeing support for you and your family.
please email Jeff at Talent@tassal.com.au

title: Northern Territory (NT)
description:
5 j
🌴 Tiwi Island Retreat 🌴 Join our Crew (2 Roles Available)
Immediate start
If you love your fishing, can work hard and get amongst it, and want to be based in one of the most unreal locations, come join our crew!
We’re about 80km off the coast of Darwin on the western edge of Bathurst Island. Tiwi Island Retreat is a proper off-grid, barefoot spot with some of the best fishing in the Top End. It’s an awesome place to call home, with a great crew to match.
We have TWO SEPARATE ROLES AVAILABLE – please read carefully and apply for the one that suits your experience.
🐟 POSITION 1: HOST (Front of House/All-Rounder)
The Role
This is a hands-on, all-rounder position where no two days are the same. You’ll be right in the mix of day-to-day operations, working closely with the Chef and other Hosts to keep everything running smoothly and guests having a great time.
What You’ll Be Doing:
* Greeting and hosting guests
* Food & beverage service
* Housekeeping and laundry
* Jumping in wherever needed across the retreat
About You:
* Easy-going, friendly and good with people
* Happy working in a remote, off-grid environment
* Team player who fits into a small, tight-knit crew
* Not afraid to get stuck in and be hands-on
* Hospitality experience
The Setup:
* Own crew accommodation + shared bathroom facilities
* Salary position (details discussed on application)
The Details:
* Seasonal role: March – November
* Can offer a firm commitment to the role for the season
* Immediate start
* No 88 days or sponsorships available
📧 Send your CV to: admin@tiwiislandretreat.com.au
🐟 POSITION 2: CHEF (Sole Chef)
The Role:
You’ll be running the kitchen solo, cooking for guests and creating a food experience that matches the location. There’s plenty of room to be creative here, especially if you love working with fresh seafood and making the most of what’s on hand.
What You’ll Be Doing:
* Preparing and cooking all guest meals
* Creating menus with creativity and flair (seafood a big focus)
* Catering for varying guest numbers throughout the season (50pax guests is absolute max capacity however the island usually has around 20-30pax as a general rule of thumb)
* Managing ordering and provisioning in a remote location
* Working closely with the Island Manager and Hosts
* Putting on special experiences like beach dinners, BBQs and guest events
About You:
* You’ve worked as a Chef in a remote or isolated setting before, or something very similar
* You understand what’s involved with provisioning in those environments (planning ahead, ordering smart, making things stretch when needed)
* Confident running a kitchen solo
* Strong seafood experience
* Organised, adaptable and easy to work with
* Keen to be part of a small, tight-knit crew
🌴This role really suits someone who’s done remote work before and enjoys that style of cooking and planning.
The Setup:
* Own crew accommodation + shared bathroom facilities
* Salary position (details discussed on application)
The Details:
* Seasonal role: March – November
* Can offer a firm commitment to the role for the season
* Immediate start
* No 88 days or sponsorships available
📧 Send your CV to: admin@tiwiislandretreat.com.au
👍 1

Paspaley Pearling Company are currently looking for Deckhands to join our Pearl Farm team in a casual role.
Our pearling operation is extensive and spans coastal regions from Exmouth, WA around to the north of Darwin, NT. Our pearl farms are positioned amongst some of the world's most spectacular coastline and unique marine environments.
You would be expected to reside in Darwin, Australia and work on a full-time basis; on a cycle of 2 weeks on, 1 week off rosters.
This is a fantastic opportunity for highly dedicated, reliable, and motivated individuals to join a multifaceted family-owned business that spans across Pearling, Retail, Pastoral and Properties.

Removal of marine growth from pearl shell
Husbandry of the shell and general shell care
Maintenance of the associated infrastructure

Consistent working history in a manual labour, marine or agricultural environment
Must be able to pass a pre-employment medical, fitness test and drug and alcohol screen
National police clearance or ability to obtain
Comfortable working in remote areas and live in close quarters
Coxswains or relevant tickets highly desirable

Attractive salary

title: Northern Territory (NT)
description:
26 août 2025
Forklift Operator - Katherine, NT
The Role
We are seeking a skilled Forklift Operator to join our client's mango pack shed in Katherine, NT. This role involves operating forklifts to move, load, and unload goods within a fast-paced packing environment.
What You'll Do
As a forklift operator, you will be required to carry out (but not limited to) the following:
Operate forklifts to load, unload, and transport goods within the mango pack shed
Support warehouse operations and product movement as required
Ensure goods are stored and handled according to operational requirements
Perform pre- and post-operation equipment checks to ensure forklifts are in safe working condition
Report any equipment issues, hazards, or maintenance needs to the supervisor
Maintain cleanliness and organisation of the work area
Benefits
Good pay rate and hours.
Eligible for visa extensions.
Opportunity for job progression
Your Skills and Experience
To be considered for the role, you will need to meet the following requirements:
Valid forklift licence with proven forklift operating experience
Experience in warehouse operations or a pack shed environment
Physically fit and able to work in a fast-paced setting
Competent and confident operating forklifts in tight spaces
Reliability and flexibility to work up to 6 days a week during peak season
To Apply:
https://www.agrilabour.com.au/candidates/current-positions/forklift-operator-katherine-nt-bbbh28314/application-form/19443/
